news_summary: Western Metallica announced the start of a first drilling program in October at its Luz Maria copper (Cu-Mo) porphyry prospect within the Caña Brava project in Peru, following permits. The news is an exploration sentiment driver with no immediate supply or demand change; nickel and aluminium are not addressed. | GGL Resources announced Teck’s option agreement for GGL’s Le Champ copper-molybdenum-gold porphyry target in Nevada, including cash option payments and a production-decision milestone. The release supports potential future copper/molybdenum development but provides no near-term output, so any immediate copper price impact is likely limited.

news_summary: Power Nickel announced assay results from the first five holes of its summer 2024 drilling at the polymetallic Lion Zone discovery. Reported intervals range from about 5–12 metres with copper-equivalent grades between 2.36% and 5.93%, which may strengthen expectations for future nickel and copper resource development. | Power Nickel reported assay results from the Lion Zone discovery, including multiple copper- and nickel-bearing intercepts (e.g., several meters of %Cu and sub-% Ni, plus CuEq). While positive drill results can support sentiment for future nickel and copper supply, the impact is likely small given the exploration stage. | Tsingshan cut Indonesia ferronickel (nickel pig iron) output due to persistent nickel ore shortages linked to delays in mining quota approvals. Nickel production costs rose and some furnaces reportedly switched to other output. Philippine ore prices jumped sharply, while Chinese NPI prices rose only modestly, implying bearish near-term nickel sentiment. | QC Copper announced an all-share acquisition of Cuprum, adding the Thierry Copper Project and increasing its copper resource base. The Thierry asset historically produced copper and contains minor nickel, with references to past concentrate shipments. The deal is positioned for future development, not immediate production or supply changes. | Orecap announced QC Copper’s offer to acquire Cuprum in an all-share deal, adding the Thierry copper project and increasing QC Copper’s copper resource base. The transaction, expected to close by end-December 2024, strengthens Canada’s copper developer footprint. Thierry also historically produced concentrate with minor nickel content.

news_summary: Nearctic Nickel Mines Inc. announced it has divested all remaining interest in Ungava Mines Inc., ending its indirect interest in UMEI. UMEI is a plaintiff in an ongoing action against the Quebec government alleging disguised expropriation. The update mainly changes company exposure, not disclosed nickel output or timing. | BHP said it will temporarily suspend Nickel West operations due to global nickel oversupply and high costs, which has pressured nickel prices. The piece argues the event could benefit Nico Resources’ Wingellina project via lower competitive pressure and investor attention, but impact on near-term nickel tightness is unclear. | Canada Nickel says it has started submitting the federal Impact Statement for its Crawford nickel sulphide project and expects to complete it within six weeks, targeting permits in 2025. The company frames the process as advancing permitting progress. This can modestly support near-term nickel market sentiment via improved supply outlook. | Prairie Operating reportedly gained additional production and increased acreage via a Nickel Road M&A transaction. The update points to improved nickel resource access and/or output potential. In the near term, incremental supply expectations can weigh on nickel pricing, with no clear information here affecting copper or aluminium.

news_summary: Power Nickel released new drill assay results from the Lion Zone at its Nisk property, highlighting longer and higher-grade intersections with notable nickel and copper grades. The company says the discovery appears substantially bigger than previously envisioned, improving confidence in mineral continuity and supporting expectations for future Ni-Cu supply. | Power Nickel reported assay results from three additional drill holes at its Lion Zone polymetallic discovery. Hole 70 returned 32m of 0.18% Ni plus 3.62% Cu and other metals, alongside additional Au and Ag. The update strengthens the perceived resource potential and is mildly supportive for nickel and copper sentiment. | Grid Metals outlines 4 km of copper-dominant mineralization with associated nickel at its MM copper/nickel project’s Eagle gabbro target in Manitoba. The company plans inaugural drilling and additional geophysical surveys. It provides grab-sample grades but no new resource estimate or production impacts. | Grid Metals reported surface copper/nickel mineralization along a ~4 km Eagle gabbro corridor at its MM project in Manitoba, with grades up to 1.3% Cu and associated nickel/cobalt/PGM values. The company plans geophysical work and inaugural drilling this fall after permits, but no production or supply changes were announced. | Granite Creek Copper reports discovery of a new “Gap Zone” at its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project in Yukon, with copper mineralization in 3 of 4 holes and results pending from sampled sections. The company suggests potential to expand resources and mine life, but this is exploration news without immediate supply impact.

news_summary: Lifezone Metals signed an MOU with JOGMEC to supply nickel from the Kabanga Nickel Project in Tanzania to Japanese battery demand. The arrangement supports JOGMEC’s acquisition of cleaner metals and references hydromet refining at Kahama. It signals incremental nickel supply visibility, with a likely supportive tone for nickel prices.

news_summary: Elliott Associates lost its appeal against dismissal of its lawsuit against the LME over billions of dollars of cancelled nickel trades. The LME annulled $12 billion in nickel trades in March 2022 after a record price spike. The Court of Appeal upheld that the LME acted lawfully in cancelling to prevent a “death spiral.” | Elliott Investment Management’s UK appeal was rejected after the LME halted a runaway short squeeze in the nickel market. The decision follows the exchange’s controversial move to stop squeeze-driven volatility. Such regulatory intervention can cool speculative pressure and weigh on nickel prices near term. | BNN Bloomberg reports that Elliott has lost a UK appeal over the LME’s nickel market shutdown. The decision highlights ongoing issues around trading or settlement of nickel on the exchange, which can worsen near-term supply/availability expectations and pressure nickel prices over coming sessions. | A Court of Appeal upheld the LME’s decision to suspend and cancel nickel trades in March 2022 after a dramatic price spike. The court said the LME acted lawfully to prevent a potential “death spiral.” The article notes nickel’s importance to stainless and batteries, but the judgment is retrospective.

news_summary: EV Nickel reported maiden drilling results at the CarLang trend, intersecting near-surface nickel mineralization in new zones (B, C and E) over more than 7 km of strike. CarLang C delivered the highest grades to date, including up to 0.96% Ni over short intervals, extending the potential resource footprint. | First Atlantic Nickel Corp. began core drilling at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland after summer sampling expanded visible awaruite (a naturally occurring nickel-iron alloy) targets along a 30 km trend. news_summary: SMM data show China’s September nickel ore imports fell 7.4% MoM and 23.5% YoY, with declines across both sulfide and oxide. Philippine ore remains the main source, but Indonesia’s RKAB quota progress expected in October may reduce Philippine demand and shift exports toward China, with possible typhoon-driven shipping delays.

news_summary: SMM reports nickel prices have fallen sharply, with SHFE dropping below 126,000 yuan/mt and LME trading around $16,300–$16,500. The main drivers are improved Indonesian nickel-ore supply expectations via RKAB approvals and broader macro risk aversion ahead of the US election. High-grade NPI remains firmer but may retreat on weakening stainless fundamentals.

news_summary: Nickel premiums were flat globally for full-plate cathodes, 4×4 cathodes, and briquettes, even as the LME three-month nickel contract hit its lowest 5pm close since mid-September. LME warehouse stocks in Rotterdam are ample and import arbitrage losses narrowed slightly. | Flying Nickel announced a final court order for an arrangement with Norway House Cree Nation to acquire the company’s Minago nickel project in Manitoba for $8 million cash and related share surrender and royalties. Closing is expected by end of October, subject to customary approvals. No operational or supply changes are detailed.

news_summary: Cumberland Resources Nickel Corp. announced a non-brokered private placement of units priced at $0.03, raising up to $750,000. Net proceeds will be used for property development and working capital related to its St. Anthony nickel property in Newfoundland. The financing could support near-term project plans, slightly improving nickel sentiment. | Indonesia’s hotel association raised concerns about a nickel project on Gag Island in Raja Ampat, arguing mining could harm the marine ecosystem and tourism. Antam’s unit bought 30% of a nickel smelter for $102 million, potentially accelerating mining. The piece highlights environmental risks more than immediate supply impacts. | QC Copper completed a cost-effective resampling program on historical drill core at the Thierry Copper Project (K1 Zone) to better delineate disseminated chalcopyrite/pyrite mineralization and potentially expand resources. Cuprum owns the project; a related business combination is expected to close in December. No immediate production or supply impacts are described. | Markets are watching for the incoming US administration to tighten IRA Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) rules, likely limiting Chinese-linked nickel from qualifying for EV tax credits. Allegations of nickel misreported as IRA-compliant are also driving scrutiny. Analysts expect automakers and battery makers to rush to secure non-FEOC nickel units, tightening supply expectations. | Nickel prices have slid toward a four-year low as weaker-than-expected electric vehicle demand and China’s real estate downturn weigh on expectations for battery-metal demand. The move highlights how softer end-demand in EVs and construction can quickly pressure nickel pricing, erasing prior gains tied to the EV boom.

news_summary: New Caledonian nickel producer Prony Resources will restart operations from Monday after a six-month shutdown linked to riots. The restart follows restored power, water and road access, though no production timeline is provided. Wider unrest has disrupted other local producers, so supply recovery could support nickel prices. | High-nickel pig iron prices are plunging further, with SMM 8-12% NPI averaging 1,005.2 yuan per nickel point and the Indonesian NPI FOB index also falling. Weak stainless demand/off-season and sluggish procurement coincide with macro pressure (Fed/LEM), pushing both NPI and pure nickel downward and widening the NPI/electrolytic nickel discount. | Indonesia’s nickel ore imports climbed to 9.3 million tons in January–October 2024, versus 161,917 tons a year earlier. Reuters links the surge to record purchases from the Philippines since April as smelter demand increased, while Indonesia delayed mining quotas amid heavy rains disrupting mining activity. | SMM reports Indonesian laterite nickel ore premiums are flat and prices stable amid uncertainty over RKAB quota consumption by year-end. In the Philippines, limited northern shipments and firmer FOB/CIF mid-grade nickel ore pricing are gradually strengthening prices, though weakening domestic high-grade NPI tempers restocking. Overall nickel ore sentiment is supportive short term.

news_summary: SMM reports Indonesia is considering changes to the non-tax state revenue (PNBP) interpretation and resource fee structure for laterite nickel ore. A proposed 2% royalty for ore below 1.5% grade is aimed at supporting battery materials. It also updates RKAB approvals and quota volumes for 2024-2026. | Xanadu Mines announced four new high-grade copper targets at its Sant Tolgoi project in Mongolia, with surface rock-chip assays up to 2.1% Cu and geophysical anomalies pointing to mineralised intrusions. The planned reconnaissance drilling will test for massive sulphide copper and possibly nickel, but no production is yet confirmed. | Electra Battery Materials received a ten-year exploration permit from the US Forest Service covering 91 drill pad locations for its Idaho Cobalt Belt projects, including the Iron Creek copper-cobalt deposit. The permit adds regulatory certainty to resume exploration, but the news is more developmental than an immediate supply change. | Xanadu Mines announced high-grade surface copper assay results from its Sant Tolgoi project in Mongolia, identifying four drill targets based on geochemistry and geophysics, with planned reconnaissance drilling in Q2 2025. Assays show copper up to 2.1% and very low nickel (<0.06%) at surface, implying exploration potential only.

news_summary: Ivanhoe Mines announced the official reopening of the Kipushi zinc-copper-germanium mine in the DRC. The restart is expected to bring additional copper supply from the existing Kamoa-Kakula/Kipushi complex over time. Ceremonial confirmation supports near-term optimism on copper output and project ramp-up. | Alaska Energy Metals reported assay results from three drill holes at the Canwell claim block, intersecting wide zones of nickel-bearing polymetallic mineralization and providing evidence of sulfide/metallic-hosted nickel. The company plans further drilling, but the update is not production-related, limiting immediate supply impact.

news_summary: A Singapore businessman, accused of running a S$1.46 billion nickel investment scam, allegedly promised profits from buying and selling physical nickel but prosecutors say no nickel trades occurred. Instead, earlier investors were paid with new funds. The case centers on fraud, money laundering, and lavish personal spending.

news_summary: A Singapore trial describes a $1.5 billion “nickel trading” scam in which 947 investors were duped after claims that nickel could be bought at a discount from an Australian mine and resold at profits. Prosecutors say no nickel was bought or sold; payments to earlier investors came from new money. | Minnesota’s DNR is postponing a decision on permits for NewRange Copper Nickel’s proposed copper and nickel mine on the Iron Range. The state has paused consideration until August next year due to regulatory and legal challenges, leaving output uncertain and potentially constraining future supply expectations. | Minnesota’s DNR is postponing its permit decision on the proposed NewRange Copper Nickel project in northeastern Minnesota, pausing further consideration until August next year. The delay stems from regulatory and legal challenges, potentially pushing back future copper and nickel supply. | Indonesia is seeking investors to fund delayed bauxite downstreaming and smelter development, after earlier plans were hindered by financing and several smelter projects were postponed. With a June 2023 ban on raw bauxite ore exports and only four of 12 smelters ready, the push to accelerate processing may support aluminium supply expectations.

news_summary: Western Australia’s nickel industry has “collapsed” this year with multiple mine closures and job losses, attributed to cheaper Indonesian nickel flooding global markets. Analysts expect nickel to remain oversupplied until at least 2029. While green/nickel-cobalt projects continue, the current market dynamics point to weak pricing near term.

news_summary: Prices for high-grade nickel pig iron (NPI) fell (SMM and Indonesian FOB). Rising Indonesian supply and reduced stainless steel spot demand during maintenance weaken purchasing, while smelter losses from ore-driven cash costs lead to output adjustments. Pure nickel faces macro headwinds and underperforming demand, keeping nickel bearish. | SMM reports Indonesian laterite nickel ore CIF/FOB premiums stable-to-weak while policy signals remain awaited. High-grade NPI prices are continuing to decline with sluggish transactions, despite rising December output. Smelters have over two months of inventory and refined nickel production is ramping up, supporting expectations of nickel oversupply.

news_summary: Reuters column says Indonesia’s nickel sector has flooded markets and drove nickel to four-year lows, but Jakarta plans to cut 2025 ore mining quotas to 200 Mt from 240 Mt. The quota outlook has coincided with a modest LME 3-month nickel rebound (+3% since early January), though inventories remain elevated. | Trump’s administration launched an “America First Trade Policy” investigation under Section 232 focused on imported steel and aluminum. news_summary: A report and experts warn that expanding nickel mining in Raja Ampat, Indonesia, is damaging a UNESCO-protected marine region. Satellite data show mining pit area grew sharply since 2020. Despite a March 2024 court ruling requiring extra protection for small islands, permits continue, raising risks to nickel production timelines. | Glencore reported 2024 output declines across several commodities, including copper. news_summary: The Nickel Institute comments on the EU “Competitiveness Compass,” arguing that EU regulatory inconsistencies—particularly revisions to nickel environmental quality standards proposed under the Water Framework Directive—could harm producers by constraining investment and competitiveness. It calls for streamlined permitting, reduced regulatory burden, and affordable energy to sustain EU nickel supply for batteries and the energy transition.

news_summary: High-nickel pig iron (NPI) prices rose this week, with SMM 8–12% NPI averaging 944.2 RMB per nickel point and the NPI FOB index also increasing. Resumed smelter operations, limited Indonesian/Philippine output, and a Philippines raw-ore export ban strengthened sentiment, narrowing the discount to electrolytic nickel. | SMM reports Indonesian domestic trade laterite nickel ore activity rebounded post-holiday, with premiums rising (1.6 grade $17–18) amid ample supply and February restocking by pyrometallurgical plants. news_summary: Trump announced a 25% tariff on all steel and aluminium imports to the US, likely including Australia, prompting diplomatic efforts for an exemption. The piece notes aluminium export volumes and likely competitive pressure on manufacturers. The tariff threat is a near-term negative for aluminium price expectations; copper and nickel aren’t directly addressed. | Asian markets were mixed, but LME base metals mostly rose after President Trump pledged 25% tariffs on all U.S. steel and aluminum imports. Aluminum futures gained 0.6% and copper 1.2% on the day, while nickel fell 0.5%. Analysts expected limited broader impact on emerging Asia exports. | Trump’s proposed 25% tariffs on all U.S. imports of steel and aluminium are rattling Asian producers by threatening higher U.S. import prices, disrupted trade flows, and lower volumes. Reports cite aluminium supply-tightening concerns and falling iron-ore sentiment, while copper and nickel are not directly addressed. | Trump says the US will impose a 25% tariff on steel and aluminium imports, with details expected soon. Australia’s industry urges a carve-out, citing past exemptions. Even with exemptions, the risk is weaker demand if tariffs slow China, pressuring overall industrial metal sentiment, especially aluminium. | Trump said the US will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminium imports starting this week, with further “reciprocal” measures. The tariff backdrop includes potential retaliation from China and warnings that tariffs will raise US costs, likely pressuring near-term industrial demand and sentiment for copper, nickel, and aluminium. | Trump signed executive orders imposing a flat 25% tariff on all steel and aluminum imports into the US “without exceptions.” The piece also discusses possible US consideration of tariff exemptions for Australia and European warnings of retaliatory tariffs. Overall, aluminum faces direct near-term price support from higher import costs. | Donald Trump says the US will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminium imports, including from Canada and Mexico, and is planning reciprocal tariffs almost immediately. The move escalates trade policy and could trigger retaliation, likely pressuring industrial metals demand expectations and prices near term. | Trump said the U.S. will impose 25% tariffs on all steel and “aluminum, too” imports, including from Canada and Mexico, with additional reciprocal duties later. Markets fell and partners warned of industrial impacts. Aluminum-focused expectations likely deteriorate near term; copper and nickel only face indirect macro/trade pressure. | Trump has signed executive orders imposing a flat 25% tariff on all US steel and aluminum imports “without exceptions or exemptions.” The news covers potential tariff exemptions for Australia, expected limited impact comments from ThyssenKrupp, and EU retaliation threats—driving near-term expectations for aluminum trade costs and prices, with only indirect effects for copper and nickel. | Trump announced executive actions raising US tariffs on aluminium and steel to 25% from 10% as China’s levies against the US take effect. Canada is the dominant source of primary aluminium imports to the US, while Mexico supplies much of the aluminium scrap and alloy. Higher tariffs likely pressure aluminium markets near term. | Trump plans to announce 25% tariffs on all imported steel and aluminum entering the U.S., including shipments from Canada and Mexico. Canada supplies major steel and is the top aluminum metal source. The move follows prior tariff threats and could tighten U.S. aluminum availability, but details remain limited. | Trump said 25% tariffs will be imposed on all steel and aluminium imports starting Monday, including shipments from Canada and Mexico. He referenced earlier 2018 tariffs and national security claims, as well as the 2019 deal that previously prevented supply surges. The new tariffs may raise costs and prices for aluminium. | Trump removed exemptions from U.S. steel tariffs so all steel imports face at least 25%, and raised 2018 aluminum tariffs to 25% from 10%. The policy change is meant to protect domestic industry and reset trade, likely increasing aluminum import costs and near-term price support. Other updates are unrelated to metal prices. | Norilsk Nickel said its 2024 IFRS net profit fell 37% to $1.8 bln as EBITDA declined and revenues dropped 13% amid weaker market prices for nickel and platinum group metals. The company reported lower free cash flow and higher net debt, while continuing investment and debt management plans. | Stocks in steel and aluminum producers jumped after President Trump said he plans 25% tariffs on imported steel and aluminum and will announce retaliatory duties on countries that impose tariffs on U.S. goods. Canada is a major exporter, contributing about 40% of U.S. aluminum imports. | Nornickel reported 2024 net profit down 37% to $1.8 billion, citing Western sanctions pressures and low prices for its key metals. The company noted payments/equipment access constraints, redirected sales to Asia, and faced repairs disruption at its Nadezhda smelter. It also warned it sees a nickel surplus in 2025. | Trump plans 25% tariffs on all US steel and aluminum imports, targeting China-linked trade flows and expanding an already tariff-heavy stance. The piece notes China’s overcapacity and rising exports depressing prices globally, which has triggered retaliation and further trade friction likely to pressure industrial metals pricing in the near term. | Trump signed executive orders imposing 25% tariffs on all US imports of steel and aluminum with no exceptions or exemptions. The US may also consider exemptions for Australian products. Europe and France signaled retaliatory measures. The tariff news is likely to raise near-term expectations for aluminum supply constraints in the US market. | President Trump orders 25% import duties on all steel and raises aluminium tariffs from 10% to 25%, covering about $50B of imports. Tariffs are expected to be passed through to U.S. buyers, pushing up prices for products using these metals and likely lifting nearby aluminium pricing pressure through higher import costs and substitution effects. | Trump signed executive orders imposing 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. from all countries, effective March 12, with Canada threatening “clear and calibrated” retaliation. The broad tariff scope includes downstream products and is expected to disrupt aluminum trade flows.

news_summary: President Trump signed proclamations expanding Section 232 tariffs on steel articles (25%) and raising aluminum import tariffs from 10% to 25%, revoking alternative arrangements and terminating product-specific exclusions. Tariffs apply to imports from all countries effective March 12, 2025, after interim impacts, aiming to protect U.S. steel and aluminum industries. | Power Nickel priced a C$40m best-efforts private placement of Quebec flow-through shares to fund Canadian exploration. Proceeds will expand work at the Nisk nickel-copper sulphide project in Quebec, including Nisk and Lion zones and targets, supporting sentiment for future nickel and copper supply. | Trump’s proclamation expands 25% tariffs on steel and increases aluminium tariffs from 10% to 25%, applying to all countries from March 12, 2025. It also revokes prior exemptions for steel and aluminium from several allies and for various derivative products. Importers must reassess supply-chain cost impacts.

news_summary: Trump issued proclamations expanding Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum imports into the US. For aluminum, the duty rate rises from 10% to 25%, exemptions and product exclusions are ended, and more “derivative” aluminum products are added within a defined mechanism effective March 12. This should support higher aluminum prices short term. | The article outlines February 10, 2025 US proclamations ending steel/aluminum tariff exemptions and exclusions and imposing across-the-board duties, including raising aluminum tariffs from 10% to 25% for most sources and extending 25% duties to additional “derivative” aluminum products from March 12, 2025. | Ontario Rivers Alliance raised concerns with Canada Nickel’s Crawford Project review, citing a lack of detail on cumulative impacts, tailings “in-process” carbon capture claims, and water/effluent management. The objections could increase regulatory uncertainty and potential project delays or added costs, which may pressure near-term nickel sentiment.

news_summary: Anglo American said it will sell its nickel business to MMG for up to $500 million, including $350 million at completion and potential earnouts/cash tied to project development. The divested assets produce about 38,000 tonnes of nickel annually. The restructuring targets a focus on copper and iron ore. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel business to MMG, covering two ferronickel operations in Brazil and related growth projects, for up to $500 million (including an initial $350 million on completion and contingent payments). Management framed it as portfolio simplification, implying limited near-term effect on nickel output. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel business to MMG for $350 million on completion, plus a $100 million price-linked earnout and $50 million contingent payment tied to final investment decisions. The assets include ferronickel operations in Brazil and greenfield projects, as part of a portfolio simplification. | Anglo American said it will sell its nickel business to China’s MMG for up to US$500 million. The transaction could shift long-term nickel supply ownership and production strategy, but it is not an immediate disruption or demand shock, so near-term nickel price impact is likely limited. | Anglo American said it will sell its nickel business to MMG for up to $500m as part of restructuring away from nickel and toward copper and iron ore. The divested assets produce about 38,000 t/year of nickel. The company expects cash at completion plus earnouts tied to development. | Anglo American agreed to sell its nickel division to MMG Singapore Resources for up to $500m, including $350m upfront and price-linked payments. The deal follows nickel selling near a four-year low, blamed on Indonesia/China supply oversupply, a stronger US dollar, and softer electric-vehicle demand. | Indonesia Battery Corporation (IBC) plans to build a nickel-based EV battery recycling plant by 2031, targeting recycling over 99% of components. The company says recycled nickel can be reused and should improve nickel availability and sustainability. While relevant to nickel, the long timeline suggests minimal near-term price impact. | Anglo American will sell its nickel business to MMG for up to $500 million, including Brazil ferronickel and development projects producing about 38,000 tonnes of nickel per year. The deal supports Anglo’s shift toward copper and iron ore, but the transfer is unlikely to immediately change physical nickel supply. | Indonesia relies heavily on Philippine nickel ore (about 97% of its imports). The deal includes cash, earnout, and development contingents. It signals portfolio shifts amid weaker Brazilian ferronickel imports to China. | Anglo American said it has agreed to sell its nickel business to MMG Singapore Resources for up to $500 million, covering two ferronickel operations in Brazil. The deal follows other divestments and is framed as cash generation, with no immediate evidence of reduced nickel output.

news_summary: First Atlantic Nickel reports a 400-meter step-out drill hole (402 meters) intersecting continuous, disseminated visible awaruite nickel-iron alloy at its RPM Zone in Newfoundland, expanding the footprint and leaving mineralization open. Phase 2 drilling is fully funded and permitted, supporting further expansion. | Alliance Nickel’s NiWest project in Western Australia has published a definitive feasibility study and claims low-cost, low-carbon nickel sulphate production with a ~10% premium over LME nickel. Despite a depressed nickel market tied to Indonesian supply, FID is not until ~Aug 2025, limiting near-term price impact. | Anglo American agreed to sell its Nickel Brazil ferronickel business (Barro Alto and Codemin) to MMG for up to $500m, with part of the consideration linked to future nickel prices. Operations are expected to transfer by Q3 2025 subject to approvals, implying limited short-term supply impact. | The Philippines is considering a ban on exporting raw nickel ore to build domestic processing capacity, potentially starting after a five-year transition. Experts say this would concern Indonesia’s smelters, as most Indonesian nickel ore imports come from the Philippines, potentially tightening supply and raising international nickel prices and processing input costs. | Eramet announced leadership changes, with Christel Bories stepping down as CEO while remaining chair, and Paulo Castellari taking over. The focus includes Eramet’s SLN nickel mine/smelter in New Caledonia, where the group stated it is no longer financing SLN. Prior remarks questioned SLN’s competitiveness versus Indonesia. | Utah family businesses say a 25% tariff on imported steel and aluminum is already prompting supplier price increases and may be passed through to their costs. A steel container maker highlights large steel input expenses, while a brewery buys many aluminum cans annually and expects higher beer pricing and tighter margins.

news_summary: VR Resources reports completion of its first winter drill program at the Silverback copper-gold project, intersecting shearing, alteration, veining and sulfide-bearing textures, and planning follow-up once assays arrive. The geological model also references nickel mineralization in mafic-ultramafic dykes, but no copper/nickel results or supply changes are provided. | The U.S. reinstated 25% tariffs on global steel imports and raised aluminum import duties from 10% to 25% effective March 12, ending prior exemptions and product exclusions. The tariffs apply to derivative products and can add duties atop existing AD/CV charges, likely weighing on aluminum-related demand expectations near term.

news_summary: Trump issued Section 232 steel and aluminum tariff proclamations effective March 12, 2025, revising duty rates and limiting relief/exclusions for covered derivative products. The measures are aimed at imported aluminum articles and can reduce import inflows and increase landed costs in the U.S., potentially supporting aluminum prices over the next weeks. | President Trump issued Section 232 proclamations reimposing blanket additional tariffs on imported aluminum (and derivative aluminum) into the US, with effects slated for March 12, 2025. The measures rescind prior exemptions/quotas and limit Commerce’s ability to grant relief, increasing uncertainty and import costs for aluminum supply. | SMM reports Indonesian nickel ore domestic trade premiums rising, with supply disruptions from rainy season, Ramadan timing, and SIMBARA enforcement tightening compliant ore flows. Active procurement after incomplete inventories is lifting premiums. Higher ore costs support high-grade NPI prices and narrow smelter losses, while refined nickel supply ramps continue. | High-grade nickel pig iron prices rose sharply amid supply constraints: nickel pig iron smelters operate at losses and some Indonesian lines run at low capacity. Demand sentiment for stainless steel is improving, traders are buying as the NPI discount versus electrolytic nickel narrows, and macro signals support pure nickel, though downstream demand remains soft.

news_summary: Reuters reports Anglo American selling its Brazilian nickel mines and plants to China’s MMG. The deal is framed against heavy nickel oversupply from Indonesia and rising LME inventories, with Class II ferronickel holding cash-flow despite LME price lows. Chinese control of refining and product shifts are highlighted. | PT Merdeka Battery Materials said it has signed agreements to build a $1.8 billion high-pressure acid leach (HPAL) plant in Indonesia. The project targets 90,000 tonnes per year of nickel materials for EV batteries, with construction starting in January and commissioning expected in about 18 months. | First Atlantic Nickel reports initial drill-hole assays from the Super Gulp Zone at its Atlantic Nickel Project in Newfoundland, finding consistent nickel mineralization across ~294 meters (up to 0.32% Ni) and ongoing DTR metallurgical testing for magnetic separation. The discovery expands a known nickel trend and supports future development expectations. | VR Resources reports maiden reconnaissance drilling at its Westwood Target (Empire Project) confirming a fertile mafic-ultramafic intrusion with sulfide segregation. Reported results include up to 0.09% Cu and 0.11% Ni at depth, alongside anomalous PGE. The findings may modestly boost expectations for future supply. | Merdeka Battery Materials, via an indirect stake in Sulawesi Nickel Cobalt, secured $1.4bn of debt financing for a new Indonesia HPAL plant on Sulawesi. The project targets 90,000 tpa of contained nickel in MHP, funded by domestic banks and supported by long-term laterite ore supply, with commissioning expected within 18 months. | The Argus Nickel Indonesia Conference highlights Indonesia’s large role in global nickel supply and forecasts growth in battery-grade nickel and nickel pig iron production. It focuses on networking and long-term commercial dealmaking across the nickel value chain. The event implies future supply/demand trends, but provides no concrete immediate market catalysts.

news_summary: High-grade nickel pig iron prices rose this week amid constrained NPI supply: smelters ran at losses with low production and Indonesia faced insufficient nickel ore feedstock. Demand improved as stainless steel mills resumed after Chinese New Year. Nickel pig iron strength supported tighter NPI vs. electrolytic nickel spreads, keeping short-term prices supported. | SMM says Indonesian laterite nickel ore premiums continue rising (though growth slows), with smelters still short of raw materials and expecting greater shipments after Sulawesi rains. Demand supports via stainless recovery and profit rebound. Indonesian DHE export FX rules may add short-term volatility. High-grade NPI hits new highs and costs ease. | Magna Mining completed its acquisition of KGHM affiliate assets in Ontario’s Sudbury Basin, including the producing McCreedy West copper mine and additional past-producing copper/nickel projects. The company will own and operate the copper mine following closing, potentially supporting near-term copper supply expectations versus demand.

news_summary: High-nickel pig iron prices rose week-on-week, with domestic supply constrained by depleted nickel ore inventories and limited Indonesian smelter output during Ramadan. Demand firmed as stainless steel plants increased production and procurement. Smelter losses eased and weaker USD plus new Indonesian ore pricing formulas supported nickel-related prices, implying continued upside risk.

news_summary: High-nickel pig iron prices (SMM 8–12%) rose week-on-week amid tight supply, low smelter inventories, and limited capacity. Indonesian NPI FOB increased and stainless steel orders strengthened, boosting purchasing. Such an incident can halt or delay operations and maintenance, reducing near-term nickel output. That supply disruption risk is bearish for nickel prices over the coming days and weeks.

news_summary: A Singapore venture capitalist testified that he invested millions into an alleged nickel scheme run by Ng Yu Zhi after checking nickel prices and being assured returns. Prosecutors say there was no real physical nickel trading; investor funds paid earlier investors. The fraud unraveled in early 2021, damaging trust and nickel-related trading confidence. | Indonesia dominates global nickel supply, with rising production over 2018–2024 and major Chinese-linked investment. Despite a recent nickel price decline linked to faster supply growth and slower EV demand, the government is considering production quota cuts (and royalty increases), which could reduce supply and lift prices. Mentions recent support from tightening expectations.

The drop is linked to weaker stainless-steel demand on macro concerns from reciprocal tariffs, cautious procurement, and expectations of continued nickel price pressure despite some supply and cost factors. | SMM’s Indonesian nickel market review says global nickel prices fell sharply on renewed Trump tariff policy and PNBP expectations, dragging down high-grade NPI prices and pressuring profits. news_summary: Indonesia implemented new non-tax royalty (PNBP) rates for nickel products effective 26 April. Rates vary by HMA-based nickel reference price for ore and are set for NPI, ferronickel, matte, and newly introduced MHP. The change is expected to raise longer-term costs but likely has limited immediate price impact.

news_summary: Brazil’s ONS denied Vale’s request to increase power supply to its northern Onca Puma nickel complex after Vale missed a deadline to contract additional electricity. The second furnace for the $555 million expansion is still targeted for the second half of 2025, but approval uncertainty raises near-term nickel supply/ramp risks.

news_summary: A Jakarta Post report says nickel mining and processing at PT Indonesia Weda Bay Industrial Park (IWIP) near Gemaf village in North Maluku causes persistent health problems, with dust and ash blamed on the plant and its coal-fired power units. news_summary: SMM’s weekly review reports a rise in Indonesian hydrometallurgical laterite nickel ore delivered prices (up ~3.8% WoW) amid weather-driven supply tightness and delayed quota approvals. news_summary: BHP will sell its 17% stake in Tanzania’s Kabanga nickel project to Lifezone for up to $83 million. Lifezone assumes full control of project off-take, while Kabanga remains pre-production.

news_summary: SMM reports nickel prices fell to a near four-year low in H1 2025, driven by macro uncertainty, surplus fundamentals, and rising LME inventories. China’s refined output and imports grew while demand recovery stayed sluggish. With H2 downside risks from US tariffs and ongoing Indonesia cost support erosion, bearish pressure persists. | Anglo American said first-half copper output fell 13% to 342,200 tonnes and cut its 2025 production outlook. It cited sluggish demand. The company is also restructuring and has agreed to sell nickel assets, but copper production guidance is the clearest potential near-term bearish pressure for prices. | Anglo American says its copper business remains on track for full-year guidance, citing higher Quellaveco throughput and improved performance at Collahuasi, alongside region-specific unit cost changes. Nickel production fell 5% due to lower grades, but 2025 guidance was unchanged. | Brixton Metals began a second drill at its Catalyst copper-gold porphyry target within the Thorn Project in NW British Columbia. The company reports a refined copper-in-soil anomaly, supportive IP geophysics, and encouraging initial rock assays, with results pending. It is exploration activity without near-term supply effects for metals.

news_summary: A Jakarta Post piece reports health impacts and heavy-metal contamination tied to nickel mining on Kabaena island, citing elevated nickel and other metals in residents’ blood and urine. It highlights weak environmental safeguards and runoff of mining waste. The implied compliance and operational risk could weigh on near-term nickel supply expectations. | Ghana terminated Rocksure’s lease for the Nyinahin bauxite deposit, ending an operator role in a planned $1.2B mine and refinery with GIADEC. news_summary: SMM’s weekly Indonesia review finds nickel ore benchmarks slightly higher, while pyrometallurgical ore demand remains cautious amid smelter negative margins. In contrast, high-nickel pig iron (NPI) prices rose, activity improved, and discounts versus electrolytic nickel narrowed, supported by stainless steel sentiment, implying mildly bullish nickel near-term.

news_summary: The Philippine Supreme Court partly rejected a VAT exemption argument by Coral Bay Nickel Corp., a PEZA-registered exporter. The government highlights SOE projects and partnerships to grow midstream battery investment and secure supply-chain efficiency. The strategy is framed around rising global EV battery demand through 2040. | Indonesia’s minister said the US will apply a 0% import tariff to Indonesian copper under the ongoing trade arrangement, improving export economics and near-term demand expectations. Negotiations continue to lower US tariffs on other commodities, including nickel, potentially from 19%. | SLAM Exploration reports additional IP survey results over its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt zones, extending deep conductive targets and identifying chargeability/resistivity anomalies east of the Granges and Logan zones. It also references prior drilling with 1.49% copper-equivalent over 64.9 meters. The update supports future potential but isn’t a current supply change. | Copper prices rose as China’s July trade data beat forecasts, lifting sentiment for industrial metals. Chinese exports and copper/copper concentrate imports increased, supporting smelter demand. news_summary: Last week’s nickel intermediate product markets stayed tight. In the MHP spot market, limited available supply led some traders to stop quoting, while downstream external demand continued to emerge and trader inventories were low. Higher cobalt sulphate and payable indicators boosted MHP costs, and rising LME nickel prices provided additional support. | MCX announced revisions to the contract specifications for its Nickel futures contracts, aiming to enhance market efficiency. The notice is about contract design rather than nickel supply, demand, inventories, or policy shocks, so it is more likely to influence trading dynamics than prompt a clear directional nickel price move.

news_summary: Tsingshan Holding Group will invest $800m to expand its Dinson Iron and Steel Company in Zimbabwe, building blast furnace and steel plants and a 50MW thermal power unit. The project aims to double carbon steel output and reduce steel imports. The nickel producer’s deeper presence may support nickel-related supply expectations, with weaker spillover to copper and aluminium. | Magna Mining reported initial drill assays from near-surface No. 1 Contact Ni-Cu zones at its Levack Mine, including grades around 2.3–2.4% Ni and 0.7–0.8% Cu over multiple metres, supporting a potential mine restart study. Near-term impact on metals prices appears limited as it is exploration-based.

It highlights US tariff package negotiations and Trump comments about access to the Indonesian nickel market, implying near-term demand sensitivity. | SMM reports tightness in the nickel intermediate product market, with strengthening payables and rising MHP prices this week as supply remains constrained and refined producers actively procure. This imbalance is described as the driver of an upward price trend, suggesting that market expectations for near-term nickel availability and conversion supply are strengthening and likely supporting nickel prices over the coming days.

news_summary: Indonesia’s ESDM issued administrative sanctions and temporarily suspended 190 mining companies over failure to provide reclamation guarantees, including multiple nickel producers. SMM says these specific firms’ quotas are small and current supply-chain impact is limited, but regulatory tightening and sentiment could support higher nickel ore prices near term. | Indonesia’s energy ministry sanctioned and temporarily suspended 25 nickel mining firms in southeast Sulawesi for inadequate reclamation and post-mining guarantees, with licence suspensions across other minerals as well. news_summary: Stellantis will end its battery-grade nickel and cobalt supply deal with Alliance Nickel’s NiWest project from Dec 3 after missed milestones. Alliance attributes delays to difficult global nickel market conditions and financing constraints as prices have stayed under pressure. The termination supports renegotiation, but implies limited immediate market-wide nickel shock. | A report says nickel mining in the Caraga region (Dinagat Islands and Surigao del Sur) has caused severe environmental degradation and community impacts, while describing nickel ore shipments largely going to China. news_summary: Ivanhoe Mines announced that South Africa’s president officially opened the Platreef mine and that the Phase 1 concentrator produced first concentrate on Nov. 18, 2025. The project targets significant platinum-group output plus payable nickel and copper by-products. Faster ramp-up supports incremental supply expectations for copper and nickel. | Ivanhoe Mines reports the official opening of its Platreef mine in South Africa and that Phase 1 concentrate production began on November 18, 2025. news_summary: Nickel prices fell on both SHFE and LME as the nickel market’s surplus widened: inventories rose to near five-year highs and SHFE broke key support around 114,050 yuan/mt. Weaker rate-cut expectations, a stronger USD, and risk-off sentiment further pressured prices, outweighing some oversold signals.

news_summary: PTX Metals began a 5,000m diamond drilling program at its W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project in Ontario. The company aims to test and expand sulfide-hosted targets using updated geophysics and preliminary metallurgy, while also citing regional infrastructure and metallurgy progress. No production or shipment figures are provided. | PTX Metals launched a 5,000m diamond drilling program at its W2 Cu-Ni-PGE project in Ontario, aiming to delineate mineralized zones and support resource and metallurgical work. It highlights ongoing surveys, geophysics, and infrastructure progress, but provides no immediate production or supply disruptions. | Copper is portrayed as facing a widening structural deficit driven by rapid demand growth from EVs, renewables and AI data centers, while mine supply gains remain limited. Disruptions, low ore grades, concentrate shortages and smelter output cuts (due to weak TCRCs) are tightening physical markets, keeping prices elevated.

news_summary: Vale Base Metals and Glencore Canada agreed to evaluate a brownfield copper development at Sudbury Basin properties using existing Glencore underground infrastructure. The project is proposed to produce about 880 kt of copper over 21 years, with final investment decision expected in 1H 2027 and engineering/permitting in 2026. Nickel would also be produced as part of polymetallic output. | Vale Base Metals and Glencore Canada agreed to jointly evaluate a brownfield copper project in Ontario’s Sudbury Basin. The plan would use existing shaft and infrastructure at Glencore’s Nickel Rim South Mine to access nearby copper deposits. Engineering and permitting are expected next year, with a final investment decision in 1H 2027. | Vale and Glencore are studying a framework for a potential $2-billion copper joint venture in Sudbury, using infrastructure at Glencore’s Nickel Rim South Mine. The plan could produce substantial copper over decades, but engineering and final investment are not until 2027, implying limited immediate supply impact.

news_summary: London LME copper surged to record highs as a weaker dollar and tight on-warrant availability drove prices higher. The LME showed large net warehouse cancellations in Asia, pushing stocks to the lowest since July. Supply worries were reinforced by Glencore cutting 2026 copper guidance, alongside gains in other metals.

news_summary: SMM reports SHFE nickel moving sideways after failing to hold a brief 118,000 yuan/mt push, closing near 117,790 yuan/mt. LME nickel ranged roughly $14,700-$14,900/mt. Spot prices and premiums rose modestly, but downstream demand stayed weak and inventories were mixed (domestic buildup, LME still high). Prices expected to remain range-bound. | SMM’s Indonesia nickel market review shows domestic nickel ore benchmarks and some delivered laterite prices falling, while NPI prices (10–12% grade) eased week-on-week. Upstream smelters/traders supported prices amid contract talks, but stainless demand is weak with production cuts, limiting upside momentum. Premiums provide some support; near-term declines are restrained. | Burundi’s President met Lifezone Metals to discuss expanding investment in the country’s mineral sector, focusing on large-scale nickel extraction. Burundi is estimated to hold about 6% of global known nickel reserves, particularly the Musongati laterite deposit. The government stressed transparency and local value creation amid rising nickel demand for batteries.

news_summary: Nornickel forecasts the global nickel market will stay in surplus at over 200,000 tonnes of Ni in both 2025 and 2026, driven by rising Indonesian supply outpacing demand growth. Indonesia’s recent licensing and quota adjustments may help but are expected to have limited short-term effect, keeping bearish pressure on nickel prices likely.

news_summary: Nickel sold off sharply, with SHFE nickel down about 2.36% and LME nickel down about 2.22% to around $14,295/mt. Spot premiums stayed high but trading was sluggish as downstream buyers turned cautious after prior stockpiling. Social inventories rose and production cuts failed to relieve inventory pressure.

news_summary: A CRI–Empower investigation says politically connected Philippine nickel exports are hard to trace after leaving the country, with limited safeguards at Chinese processing hubs in Indonesia. Communities report pollution-linked harm amid ASEAN EV growth and rising nickel demand. The piece raises compliance and reputational risks rather than immediate nickel supply disruption. | Canada Nickel announced initial mineral resource estimates for its Midlothian and Bannockburn nickel sulphide projects near Timmins, Ontario, reporting inferred and indicated resources totaling substantial contained nickel. While this improves the company’s long-term resource base, it is unlikely to materially alter near-term nickel supply expectations within weeks. | The ILO warns the Philippines could miss green-transition benefits without reforms to improve “decent work” and domestic value creation in its nickel supply chain. While nickel demand should rise, the industry says further processing is limited by low LME nickel prices and higher costs versus Indonesia/China.

news_summary: SMM reports Bloomberg citing APNI sources that Indonesia may cut its 2026 nickel ore RKAB quota by about 34% to ~250 million tons to tighten supply and support nickel prices. While Indonesia’s 2025 output already ran below quota, rising HPAL/processing demand and declining ore grades could lift ore needs. | The Democratic Republic of Congo suspended artisanal copper processing and ordered entities to halt operations and certify mineral origins amid a corruption crackdown. The decree aims to improve transparency and prevent illegal exports. While cobalt supply issues are prominent, the article provides no quantified estimate of copper output changes or price effects.

news_summary: The DRC Ministry of Mines partially and temporarily lifted a Dec. 19 suspension on copper-cobalt processing after a compliance review in Lualaba Province. Processing entities operating exclusively in Lualaba can resume, and Haut-Katanga firms may process stockpiled minerals under supervision, pending inspections. The move targets OECD due-diligence compliance and traceability.

A strike at Capstone’s Mantoverde mine in Chile and delays to Tongling’s Ecuador project were cited. Nickel gains were reinforced by Indonesia plans to cut ore output. Aluminium rose as well. | Aluminum surged above $3,000/t on the LME, with buyers citing enforced China primary output caps, high European smelter energy costs, and “green aluminum” premiums tightening LME-grade availability. The rally is echoed by record copper and improved nickel prices, reflecting electrification-driven demand and a carbon-border-driven supply vacuum.

news_summary: Nickel prices surged to a 15-month high after Vale halted operations in Indonesia. The stoppage tightens short-term supply from Indonesia, a key low-cost nickel producer used in stainless steel. With oversupply previously pressuring the market, the disruption provides immediate support, boosting price expectations for nearby weeks. | Nickel prices surged on 6 January, with LME nickel above $18,000/t and SHFE hitting its daily limit, supported by robust sentiment and possible changes to Indonesia’s mining plans. Market discussion centers on an RKAB cut and potential export-benchmark/tariff mechanisms, while copper strength also lifted nickel.

news_summary: SMM reports nickel and SHFE nickel futures have risen, creating structural arbitrage versus non-standard high-grade NPI prices. Despite off-season stainless demand and high NPI inventories, upstream and downstream are executing more high-priced trades (980–995 yuan/mtu). With ore costs lower and potential stainless price recovery, NPI acceptance may rise toward 980 yuan/mtu within a month. | Perseverance Metals reported additional drill assays from the Baseline Zone at the Lac Gayot project, including a 2.1m massive sulphide intersection with high nickel and copper grades, alongside cobalt and PGE values. Management highlights expanding conductive targets for follow-up drilling. While the results may modestly support copper-focused market sentiment, there is no indication of near-term changes to mined output, inventories, or refined metal flows.

news_summary: First Atlantic Nickel links its Pipestone XL awaruite nickel-cobalt alloy discovery to a January 14 White House proclamation targeting U.S. processed critical-mineral import vulnerabilities, highlighting nickel’s downstream processing dependence. The company argues awaruite could be beneficiated without smelting into nickel sulfate feed, potentially easing near-term nickel processing constraints.

The article notes copper prices have firmed above $13,000/t and gold is near records, with plans to update resources and begin early studies. news_summary: Chinese regulators’ crackdown on high-frequency trading aimed at reducing volatility and leveling access for retail investors led to a weekend speed bump in base-metal prices on the LME. The article says copper and nickel’s early-year rally hit a pause, indicating near-term sentiment/liquidity effects rather than fundamentals. | Chinese regulators have cracked down on high-frequency traders in base metals on the London Metal Exchange, aiming to reduce market imbalance and volatility. After a strong early-year run, the policy shift caused a weekend speed bump, weighing on copper and nickel prices and potentially altering short-term trading conditions.

news_summary: PT Vale Indonesia is appealing Indonesia’s approval of its annual production plan (RKAB) after receiving only about 30% of the nickel ore quota it requested. The shortfall may jeopardize supply commitments for three HPAL plants, including a major Pomalaa project, creating potential near-term nickel supply uncertainty. | First Quantum reported 2025 copper production in Zambia fell 8% to 370,000 tons but set out a steady rebound. Kansanshi volumes are guided higher into 2026–2028, driven by ramp-up of the S3 processing circuit and improved ore grades. Sentinel remains weaker on lower-grade ore and maintenance. | Sarytogan Graphite reports shallow drilling at Kazakhstan’s Baynazar project found a 600m-wide copper bedrock anomaly up to 0.5% Cu, with copper mineralisation confirmed by malachite and sulphide veinlets. Alteration patterns fit a copper porphyry system, supporting expectations for potential future copper resources.

news_summary: MacroAsia’s subsidiary received Mines and Geosciences Bureau clearance to develop and operate the Infanta Nickel Project in Palawan. The approval authorizes extraction and commercial disposition of nickel (and associated minerals) under MPSA terms, subject to environmental and regulatory conditions, after an ECC was issued in December 2023.

news_summary: Westwin Elements is seeking a site in Richmond Hill, Georgia, to build a large nickel refinery producing Class 1 nickel for defense and energy security. The plan faces environmental, legal, and community opposition, and a previous similar effort stalled amid weaker nickel prices. Still, U.S. supply support is bullish for nickel sentiment. | Fathom Nickel announced a C$3 million private placement to fund resumed drilling at its Gochager Lake Ni-Cu-Co project, with Crescat taking a strategic stake. The company cites a strengthening nickel market and plans a 3,000–4,000 meter program, but provides no immediate supply or inventory impact. | Abitibi Metals announced additional high-grade copper-gold intercepts from Phase 3 drilling at the B26 polymetallic deposit in Québec, including 12.83% CuEq over 5.0 metres. The company is expanding drilling into Phase 4 and highlights resource growth potential, implying incremental bullish sentiment for copper, but no confirmed near-term supply change.

news_summary: Nickel prices extended gains, with SHFE 2602 up 2.52% WoW and LME 3M up 4.07%, supported by positive macro news and Indonesian developments. Spot trading was sluggish and average spot prices edged down, while domestic social inventory rose, yet the outlook says nickel should hold up next week. | SLAM Exploration began an 852 km VTEM-Plus airborne survey over its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project in New Brunswick. The company aims to map potential copper-nickel targets based on prior drill intercepts. Assays are pending for a separate gold program; no production or inventory changes are announced. | SLAM Exploration started an 852 km VTEM-Plus survey over its Goodwin copper-nickel-cobalt project in New Brunswick to refine targets for potential ovoid-type Cu-Ni deposits. The release cites strong historical copper-nickel-equivalent drilling but provides no new assay results or production/inventory impacts. | Prospect Ridge reports final IP survey results identifying a large chargeability/resistivity/magnetic target at its Excalibur copper-gold porphyry project and expects to finalize drill targets and begin a maiden diamond program in June 2026, subject to permits. The update is sentiment-supportive for copper but no near-term supply effect is stated.

news_summary: Westwin Elements is seeking to build what it says would be the only major U.S. nickel refinery in coastal Georgia, scaling from 18,000 to 68,000 tons annually and producing Class 1 nickel for defense and energy security. The project faces local environmental and legal opposition, but could boost expectations for domestic nickel supply. | Bryan County, Georgia residents are split over Westwin Elements’ proposal to build a nickel refinery at an abandoned industrial site. Opponents cite environmental and safety risks; the company argues strong containment and compliance, using nickel carbonyl in a sealed process. A town hall is scheduled to review project data. | First Atlantic Nickel reports expanded RPM Zone drilling at its Pipestone XL project, with all holes returning positive magnetically recoverable nickel (awaruite) results and continued growth of the nickel-bearing footprint. It also references a White House proclamation on US imports of processed critical minerals, naming nickel and cobalt amid limited domestic processing capacity. | Quimbaya Gold reports independent confirmation of an extensive Tahami porphyry copper-gold-molybdenum system, with visible copper and anomalous Cu/Mo in preliminary sampling. The company plans maiden drilling in Q2 2026, but there is no near-term production or supply change, limiting immediate price pressure. | Benton Resources announced new VMS-style copper intersections at its South Pond project in Newfoundland, including up to 1.6% Cu over 0.70 m, and additional gold results. One drill hole also found highly anomalous nickel, chromium, and cobalt intervals, with follow-up drilling planned. This is exploration news with limited near-term price impact.

news_summary: True North Copper increased its Wallace North copper-gold mineral resource in Queensland by adding results from two RC drillholes, taking contained copper to about 25,000 tonnes and gold to about 50,000 ounces. The update supports ongoing studies and extension drilling, reinforcing bullish sentiment around near-term copper supply expectations. | EV Nickel announced positive interim flotation test results from its Gemini North nickel sulphide zone. Tests show nickel recoveries of ~81–85% and final concentrate grades meeting project targets (up to ~5.83% Ni, with variability to 9.70% noted), using repeatable conditions suitable for bioleaching. | Fathom Nickel upsized a private placement to fund a planned 3,000–4,000 m drill program at its Gochager Lake Ni-Cu-Co project, targeting sulphide mineralization and using BHEM and soil geochemistry. The company provides nickel and copper tenors but no near-term production or supply changes.

news_summary: Indonesia (ESDM) cut PT Weda Bay Nickel’s 2026 production quota to 12 million Mwmt, a ~71% drop from the prior year, aiming to constrain supply and stabilize prices. LME nickel surged over 2% after the announcement, shifting the market narrative from surplus to imminent shortage.

news_summary: Copper’s historic rally abruptly reversed on March 4, with LME prices falling nearly 10% as investors de-risk amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions. The blockade boosts energy costs and interrupts sulfur supply for SX-EW, raising costs while geopolitics lifts war-risk premiums. Demand-growth fears and de-grossing deepen the bearish move. | Debswana approved plans to diversify beyond diamonds amid a demand slump, including expanding copper and platinum projects via Naledi Mining Services and developing a solar farm near the Jwaneng mine. The article suggests strategic redirection of investment and potential long-run supply signals, but no immediate production changes are specified.

African copper miners may face similar shortages and higher acid costs. | Boliden said its Kevitsa mine expansion in Finland is on hold after Finland quadrupled mining taxes on metal ores from 0.6% to 2.5%. The changes could affect operations at the Kevitsa copper and nickel producer, raising supply-growth risk. | Research cited by TASS says Russian shipments to China surged in 2025: aluminium up 94% to 2.1 mln tons, nickel up 51% to 88,000 tons, and refined copper up 58% with copper concentrate up 2.1-fold. news_summary: Four GEM-linked Chinese-run HPAL nickel plants at Indonesia’s Morowali park have shut temporarily after a deadly landslide at a waste site. The biggest QMB unit may stay offline up to three months. The disruptions hit roughly 30% of local HPAL capacity as Indonesia increases environmental enforcement.

news_summary: Researchers at Cornell report a nickel-on-carbon catalyst that enables high-performance alkaline fuel cells without precious metals like platinum. By improving nickel’s stability in alkaline media, the work could eventually expand fuel-cell applications using nickel. However, it does not signal near-term deployment, so short-term price impact is likely limited.
